---
title: Finishing with boiled linseed oil
date: 2024-04-30
tags: [woodworking, finishing]
---

Boiled linseed oil is the forgiving finish. It penetrates into the wood rather than sitting on top, deepens the grain without plasticizing it, and any scratches can be repaired by wiping on more oil years later.

My routine is three coats. I flood the surface with oil, let it sit for fifteen minutes, and wipe off every drop that has not soaked in. A second coat goes on the next day and a third the day after that. Between coats I knock back any raised grain with a worn piece of brown paper bag, which is gentler than sandpaper.

The only real danger is spontaneous combustion in oily rags. I spread them flat on the concrete floor outside until they are crisp, or drop them in a sealed can of water. More than one workshop has burned down because a crumpled oily rag was tossed in a bin overnight.
